config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because that can easily become an excuse for abuse by those in power. From my perspective, laws should protect both public welfare and individual dignity, but when they clash, basic rights should not be sacrificed lightly. In my experience, a society is healthier when it respects personal freedom while still asking people to contribute to the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami justru penuh pelajaran bahwa ketika negara atau mayoritas diberi terlalu banyak kuasa atas nama “kebaikan bersama,” hasilnya sering merugikan kebebasan sipil dan kelompok minoritas. Tentu masyarakat butuh aturan untuk keselamatan umum, tetapi pembatasan hak harus sangat sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dijadikan alasan umum untuk mengorbankan kebebasan pribadi. Bagi saya, hak individu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; justru itu fondasi agar masyarakat tetap adil dan tidak sewenang-wenang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should have automatic priority over the individual, because in Indonesia we have seen how “public interest” can be used to silence criticism, excuse corruption, or pressure minorities. At the same time, I also do not believe individual rights should be treated as absolute in every case, because a society still needs discipline, solidarity, and rules to protect others. The real test is whether a restriction is truly necessary, proportionate, and transparent, not whether it simply sounds good for the majority. For me, a healthy society protects the common good best when it does not forget the dignity and rights of each person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika “kepentingan umum” dipakai terlalu longgar, itu sering berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk penyalahgunaan kuasa, sensor, dan penekanan minoritas. Memang benar bahwa aturan publik kadang perlu membatasi kebebasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan bisa diuji, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa itu praktis. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang paling serius melindungi martabat dan kebebasan setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a blanket rule, because in Indonesia that idea is too easy to abuse by officials or majorities who claim to speak for everyone. At the same time, I also do not support an extreme individualism that ignores social duties, because our communities depend on mutual responsibility and public order. What matters is whether any limit on rights is truly necessary, narrowly targeted, and accountable, not whether it is justified with vague talk about the common good. For me, a society is strongest when it protects both collective welfare and the dignity of each person, instead of treating one as disposable for the other.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara otomatis di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa mudahnya “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk melegitimasi sensor, penyalahgunaan kuasa, dan penindasan minoritas; itu alasan yang terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam orang. Memang, masyarakat butuh aturan dan kadang hak individu bisa dibatasi, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sangat sempit, perlu, dan bisa diawasi, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa lebih nyaman. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang paling serius melindungi kebebasan dan martabat setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
